lesson.name: God is Dead, Nihilism & the Übermensch - Lesson 4: The Übermensch

meta.courseTitle: God is Dead, Nihilism & the Übermensch
meta.lessonObjective: Test the misread concept of the Übermensch against Zarathustra.

tasks.chapterTitle: Part II: Discourses and Bestowing Virtue
Part III: Grave-Song and Sublime Ones
The Spirit of Gravity and Old/New Tables
The Convalescent and The Seven Seals
The Song of Zarathustra and Final Discourses

messages.content:
Lesson 4: The Übermensch - Presentation27 Slides
Guiding Quote: I Teach You the Superman
  • “I teach you the Superman. Man is something that is to be surpassed.”
  • The Übermensch (rendered “Superman” in this translation) is no master race and no tyrant, but a self-overcoming: one who creates new values after the death of God.
  • This lesson tests the widely misread concept against Zarathustra’s own words.
Test your understanding of this lesson:

questions.question: What does Zarathustra seek instead of herds or believers?
options: Fellow-creators who grave new values on new tables
The good and just who keep old tables
The marketplace and its noise
The cripples who need healing

questions.question: Which stage of the spirit wins freedom for new creating?
options: The camel that bears the heaviest load
The lion that says sacred No to Thou shalt
The child that begins anew
The dragon that says all values already exist

questions.question: What does Zarathustra desire after God dies?
options: The return of the old tables
The happiness of the greatest number
The Superman to live
Submission and humility

questions.question: What must the higher men do according to Zarathustra?
options: Become masters of the petty people by policy
Submit to the sand-grain considerateness of today
Maintain themselves pleasantly and longest
Be surpassed so that man grows aloft

questions.question: How does Zarathustra describe redemption?
options: Transforming every It was into Thus would I have it
Healing the blind and making the lame run
Accumulating riches for personal safety
Flying away from the earth to eternal walls

cards.front: What does Zarathustra show his companions?
cards.back: The rainbow and all the stairs to the Superman.

cards.front: Who does the creator seek?
cards.back: Fellow-creators, reapers, and rejoicers who grave new values.

cards.front: What does the lion achieve?
cards.back: Freedom for new creating by saying sacred No to the dragon.

cards.front: What is the highest virtue?
cards.back: A bestowing virtue that is uncommon, unprofiting, and beaming.

cards.front: What does Zarathustra desire after God dies?
cards.back: That the Superman live.

cards.front: What question does Zarathustra ask first?
cards.back: How is man to be surpassed?

cards.front: What is redemption?
cards.back: Transforming every It was into Thus would I have it.

cards.front: What must one overcome to become light?
cards.back: The spirit of gravity and its extraneous heavy words.

cards.front: What is Zarathustra ardent for?
cards.back: Eternity and the marriage-ring of the return.

cards.front: Why must higher men still be surpassed?
cards.back: They remain fragments until they create new values.

slides.title: Guiding Quote: I Teach You the Superman
bullets: “I teach you the Superman. Man is something that is to be surpassed.”
The Übermensch (rendered “Superman” in this translation) is no master race and no tyrant, but a self-overcoming: one who creates new values after the death of God.
This lesson tests the widely misread concept against Zarathustra’s own words.

slides.title: Zarathustra Announces the Superman
bullets: Zarathustra declares he will show companions the rainbow and all the stairs to the Superman.
He seeks fellow-creators who grave new values on new tables rather than herds or believers.
The stairs demand that each companion first shatter every inherited measure of worth inside himself, otherwise the climb merely repeats the old errors under a new name.
Such creators must therefore endure the solitude that follows the breaking of all communal tablets, knowing the Superman appears only where the herd instinct has already been overcome.
slides.notes: Direct from prologue on p.18.

slides.title: The Creator Seeks Fellow-Creators
bullets: The creator seeks companions who are reapers and rejoicers, not corpses or believers.
Zarathustra parts from the dead and will associate only with creators who know how to whet their sickles.
Only among such active equals can the creator's harvest avoid turning into another dead doctrine that future generations merely inherit without renewing.

This refusal of passive company therefore exposes the solitary Übermensch as a misreading, since genuine creation demands living partners who will themselves destroy and affirm beyond any single teacher.
slides.notes: Prologue sets the goal of the teaching on p.18.

slides.title: The Superman as Goal of Down-Going
bullets: Zarathustra follows his course over the loitering and tardy so that his on-going becomes their down-going.
He makes for his goal and leaps over those who remain behind.
This onward leap therefore turns the many into stepping-stones whose very failure supplies the tension and contrast out of which the higher type can first become visible.
The price of such passage is that pity must be refused, for any halt to rescue the stragglers would betray the goal and leave the human all too human unchallenged.
slides.notes: Emphasizes the directional aim on p.18.

slides.title: The Three Metamorphoses Begin the Path
bullets: The spirit first becomes a camel that takes the heaviest burdens upon itself in the wilderness.
In the loneliest wilderness the spirit becomes a lion that captures freedom and says sacred No to the dragon Thou shalt.
The lion's sacred negation therefore clears space but cannot yet fill it, leaving the spirit suspended between the ruins of old commands and the demand for something still unborn.

Only when the spirit descends further into the child does the path open toward the Übermensch, because creation now arises from playful affirmation rather than from the memory of what had to be destroyed.
slides.notes: Foundational sequence on p.19.

slides.title: The Lion Creates Freedom for New Values
bullets: The lion cannot yet create new values but wins the freedom for new creating by struggling with the dragon.
The dragon says all values have already been created and there shall be no I will any more.
This victory therefore leaves the lion standing in a cleared space where every old command has been torn down, yet the same clearing shows that negation alone cannot fill the future with meaning.
The lion thus discovers that the freedom it has seized is only a threshold, and that the real labor of the Übermensch still lies ahead in the act of willing something never before affirmed.
slides.notes: Lion stage directly prepares the Superman on p.19.

slides.title: The Child Completes the Metamorphoses
bullets: After the lion the spirit becomes a child, innocent and forgetful, a new beginning.
Only the child can say sacred Yes and create new values as a wheel rolling out of itself.
This forgetful innocence therefore turns the lion’s hard-won freedom into an ongoing festival of invention rather than a single victorious moment.
Yet because every new value will itself grow heavy, the child’s wheel must keep rolling, or the spirit will sink back into the camel’s burden.
slides.notes: The child is the final stage named on p.19.

slides.title: Bestowing Virtue as Highest Virtue
bullets: Gold came to highest value because it is uncommon, unprofiting, beaming, and always bestoweth itself.
A bestowing virtue is the highest virtue and the glance of the bestower is goldlike.
The one who bestows therefore stands outside all calculation of gain or loss, turning every act of giving into a fresh source of light that draws others upward without ever demanding repayment.

Yet this same overflow creates a sharp divide: those unable to receive without envy or to give without resentment remain chained to the marketplace, while the bestower alone founds a new order of rank.
slides.notes: Links virtue to the creator type on p.42.

slides.title: Remain True to the Earth
bullets: Zarathustra conjures his disciples to remain true to the earth with the power of their virtue.
Let bestowing love and knowledge give the earth a human meaning instead of flying away to eternal walls.
Such fidelity turns the body into the sole arena where virtue can create rather than deny, so that every act of bestowing becomes an earthly redemption rather than a postponed promise.

The danger is that the old longing for eternal walls lingers inside the very will to create, ready to invert love and knowledge into new instruments of flight unless the disciple continually re-anchors them in flesh and soil.
slides.notes: Earthly orientation of the new creator on p.43.

slides.title: The Superman Lives After God Dies
bullets: God hath died and now we desire the Superman to live.
Only since God lay in the grave have the higher men again arisen.
With the divine throne empty the task of value creation falls squarely upon human shoulders so that every act of self overcoming now carries the weight once borne by commandments from above.
This shift means the higher men must continually renew their own standards in the face of nothingness or else sink back into the very mediocrity the dead God once sheltered.
slides.notes: Direct statement in part IV on p.136.

slides.title: The Higher Men Must Be Surpassed
bullets: Zarathustra asks how man is to be surpassed rather than how man is to be maintained.
The Superman is the first and only thing at heart, not the neighbour or the poorest.
Even those who first embody the leap beyond the herd must themselves be left behind, otherwise their achieved form hardens into a new limit that stops the very movement it once released.
The result is an unending ladder on which every higher type becomes, in turn, the next thing to be overcome, so that no station of strength or beauty is permitted to settle into permanence.
slides.notes: Central question on p.136.

slides.title: Over-Going and Down-Going in Man
bullets: What can be loved in man is that he is an over-going and a down-going.
In the higher men there is much that makes Zarathustra love and hope.
This double movement means that any true advance in strength or insight requires the deliberate ruin of what one has already become, so that nothing fixed or comfortable can block the path ahead.

The higher men therefore stand as living bridges whose value lies not in their present state but in their willingness to shatter themselves, turning personal ruin into the only soil from which something greater can still grow.
slides.notes: Criterion for loving man on p.136.

slides.title: Despair Rather Than Submit
bullets: The petty people preach submission and have become master, yet ye higher men know not how to live today.
Rather despair than submit yourselves to the sand-grain considerateness of the ant-hill.
Yielding to the ant-hill flattens the inner distance required for self-overcoming and turns even the strongest into caretakers of mediocrity.
Despair therefore functions as a deliberate holding-open of possibility, refusing the comfort that would erase the higher man before he can become what he is.
slides.notes: Rejection of petty virtues on p.136.

slides.title: The Evilest Is Necessary for the Superman
bullets: Man is evil and the evilest is necessary for the Superman's best.
Man must become better and eviler so that the great despisers remain great reverers.

Only when man stops shielding himself from his own worst drives does the raw material for a higher type actually appear, because those drives alone supply the pressure that forces an individual beyond the herd.

This means the very people who learn to despise what is small and safe must also keep their reverence intact, or else their contempt collapses into mere destruction and never opens a path for the Superman.
slides.notes: Revaluation of evil on p.136.

slides.title: Always More and Harder Trials
bullets: Always more, always better ones of your type shall succumb, for ye shall always have it worse and harder.
Only thus groweth man aloft to the height where the lightning striketh and shattereth him.
This demand ensures that every advance immediately exposes its own limits, so the strongest must invite their own replacement rather than settle into any achieved form.
The resulting isolation becomes concrete when companions fall away, leaving the climber without witnesses or aid precisely when the next, harsher test arrives.
slides.notes: Demand for greater severity on p.136.

slides.title: The Superman as Bridge to the Future
bullets: Zarathustra walks among men as fragments of the future and a bridge to the future.
His poetisation is to compose what is fragment and riddle and fearful chance into unity.
This means the present must be endured as raw material whose discord already strains toward a form no current eye can yet hold steady.

The tension arises because every step across the bridge risks shattering the fragments before unity can take hold, leaving only the creator’s will to decide whether chance yields a future or collapses back into the old rubble.
slides.notes: Redemption image on p.72.

slides.title: Redemption Transforms It Was
bullets: To redeem what is past and transform every It was into Thus would I have it is called redemption.
Will is the emancipator, yet the will itself remains a prisoner of It was.
Only when the will learns to recreate the unalterable sequence of events as the very expression of its own desire does it cease gnawing at the chain of time.
Failing this creative reversal, every forward stride remains secretly governed by the backward glance that cannot forgive what once occurred.
slides.notes: Core act of the creator on p.72.

slides.title: The Spirit of Gravity Must Be Overcome
bullets: The spirit of gravity loads man with extraneous heavy words and worths so that life seems a desert.
He who would become light and be a bird must learn to love himself with wholesome love.

Only when this wholesome self-love takes root does the desert bloom into a realm where the creator dares to dance his own values into being, turning every burden into a flight that affirms life without borrowed anchors.

Yet the ascent carries its own peril, because the lightest wings still feel the pull of yesterday's judgments, and without ceaseless vigilance the bird risks folding back into the very gravity it once escaped.
slides.notes: Hostility to gravity on p.95-96.

slides.title: Learn to Love Yourself as a Bird
bullets: One must learn to love oneself so that one may endure to be with oneself and not go roving about.
Such roving christens itself brotherly love and has been the best lying and dissembling.
This endurance turns solitude into the workshop where one fashions values that owe nothing to the applause or pity of the herd.
The bird that circles alone discovers its own wingbeat; those who cannot bear the same height keep inventing neighbors to distract them from the drop.
slides.notes: Healthy selfishness on p.96.

slides.title: The Great Noontide Reveals the Ego
bullets: At the great noontide many things shall be revealed and he who proclaims the ego wholesome and holy speaks what he knoweth.
Behold, it cometh, it is nigh, the great noontide.
At that hour the old war within the self finally ends, because every drive once branded sinful now stands justified by its own strength and necessity.
Yet the same light that sanctifies the ego also isolates the one who speaks for it, since neighbors still measure worth by the very renunciations now declared obsolete.
slides.notes: Climax of self-affirmation on p.95.

slides.title: Eternal Return as Marriage Ring
bullets: Zarathustra is ardent for eternity and for the marriage-ring of rings, the ring of the return.
He loves eternity because only through it can the will redeem all It was.
This ring therefore forces the will to turn backward and sanctify every 'it was' as the very condition of its own future strength, so that no deed can ever be disowned without breaking the circle.

The demand reveals a hidden cost: only a creator strong enough to desire the eternal repetition of his own errors can truly stand as the one who overcomes man, turning redemption into an unending wedding vow rather than a final escape.
slides.notes: Seven seals motif on p.114.

slides.title: Everything Heavy Shall Become Light
bullets: If my Alpha and Omega is that everything heavy shall become light, every body a dancer, every spirit a bird.
Then could I not be ardent for eternity and the ring of return.

This demand for lightness means the Übermensch must first learn to bear the full weight of time and body without resentment, turning that very weight into the rhythm of dance rather than its opposite.
Only when every burden has been transfigured into flight does the thought of eternal return cease to terrify and instead become the highest object of desire.
slides.notes: Dancer motif on p.114.

slides.title: The Superman Requires New Tables
bullets: The breaker of tables is the creator who seeks fellow-creators to grave new values.
The good and just hate most the law-breaker because he brings new tables.
This search for fellow creators turns the solitary act of destruction into a deliberate gathering, where the breaker must test others to see who can endure the weight of values still unwritten.

The resulting enmity from the good and just therefore functions as a proving ground, sharpening the new tables by forcing their creator to stand apart until those tables can command loyalty beyond the herd.
slides.notes: Value creation on p.18.

slides.title: Misreading the Superman as Mere Ideal
bullets: The Superman is not a fixed type to be worshipped but the name for the one who creates freedom for new values.
Zarathustra shows stairs, not a finished portrait, because the child alone begins anew.
Treating the Superman as a finished model to copy turns the very freedom it names into a new obedience, so that the creator’s task is replaced by imitation and the future is closed before it opens.
Because the child must begin anew, every image of the Superman must be overcome in turn, otherwise the stairs themselves harden into the idol that Zarathustra came to destroy.
slides.notes: Corrects static readings using p.18-19.

slides.title: The Superman Lives Only Through Creators
bullets: Without the lion that says I will and the child that says sacred Yes there is no Superman.
The higher men must still be surpassed because they remain fragments until they create.
Only when the rebel who shatters old values also risks the solitude of inventing new ones does the Superman step out of mere longing into actual existence.
If the higher men cling to their partial victories without completing the work of creation, the human type simply repeats its old contradictions and the future stays closed.
slides.notes: Dynamic process on p.136 and p.19.

slides.title: Quiz · Self-test
